game_config.description: Token-free game config used for certification and default local episode runs.
game_config.title: Game Config

players.description: Ordered bundled-player slots for the certification episode.
players.title: Players

player_id.description: ID of a bundled player runnable to use for this certification slot.
player_id.title: Player Id

readme.description: Required top-level README.md document for game rules, strategy, player guidance, and FAQs.
readme.title: Readme

pages.description: Optional public documentation pages. Use game-authored pages for supplemental material; Softmax leagues may also list a platform-owned play_*.md guide for setup, upload, and submission.
pages.title: Pages

version.description: Coworld package version. Pydantic validation requires a valid PEP 440 version.
version.title: Version

description.description: Human-readable game description surfaced by product UIs.
description.title: Description

config_schema.description: JSON Schema for runtime game configs. It must require a string-array `tokens` field for runner-injected player auth with minItems/maxItems validity bounds; variants and certification configs omit tokens because the runner injects them.
config_schema.title: Config Schema

results_schema.description: JSON Schema for the game-written results artifact. Cross-game consumers require `scores`.
results_schema.title: Results Schema

runnable.description: Game container runnable. Its `type` must be `game`.

protocols.description: Protocol documentation for game-owned WebSocket surfaces.

docs.description: Game-authored documentation surfaced through Coworld tools and UIs.

promo.description: Optional promotional material for the game. When set, product UIs (e.g. the Observatory league page) surface a Video Promo tab for the promo video.

type.description: Manifest role section this runnable belongs to.
type.title: Type

image.description: Docker image reference to run for this role.
image.title: Image

run.description: Optional process command overriding the image entrypoint or default command.
run.title: Run

env.description: Public environment variables passed to the runnable. Do not put secrets here.
env.title: Env

source_url.description: Optional public source repository, directory, or file URL for this runnable.
source_url.title: Source Url

resources.description: Optional compute request profile for this runnable. Omit to inherit the role's default profile. Declared requests are clamped to per-role bounds by the backend.

id.description: Stable runnable identifier within this manifest. Certification fixtures reference player ids.
id.title: Id

name.description: Human-readable runnable name for CLIs and UIs.
name.title: Name

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this runnable does.
description.title: Description

repository_url.description: Optional Git repository the local optimizer tooling clones and runs. Used by `coworld optimize` to resolve a game-specific optimizer workbench; informational for other roles.
repository_url.title: Repository Url

video_url.description: Public HTTP(S) URL for a promotional video. Product UIs (e.g. the Observatory league page) surface a Video Promo tab that embeds this URL.
video_url.title: Video Url

engine_runtime.description: Optional canonical engine runtime identifier for this game. Supported values are `mettagrid`, `cogweb`, `bitworld`, and `nimgrid`.
engine_runtime.title: Engine Runtime

CoworldReporterPlatformReference.description: Reference to an already-submitted platform reporter version (spec 0061).

reporter.description: Platform reporter version reference, "owner/name@version". Reporter names are unique per owner, so the owner (the owning user's id) qualifies the name.
reporter.title: Reporter

CoworldReporterPlatformReference.title: CoworldReporterPlatformReference

CoworldReporterWasmReference.description:
A wasm component this package builds; `coworld upload-coworld` submits it
through the standard reporter upload flow (creating or extending a platform
reporter named `{coworld-name}-{id}` owned by the submitter — names cannot
contain "/", the owner separator) and rewrites the entry to an owner-qualified
platform reference before the manifest is uploaded (spec 0061).

wasm.description: Package-relative path to a wasm component targeting the softmax:reporter world.
wasm.title: Wasm

id.description: Reporter id; the platform name becomes {coworld-name}-{id}.
id.title: Id

display_name.description: Human-facing title shown on the Reporters list. Defaults to a humanized `id` (e.g. 'round-recap' -> 'Round Recap') when omitted.
display_name.title: Display Name

description.description: Long-form description shown on the Reporters list. Defaults to `attributes.purpose` when omitted.
description.title: Description

attributes.description: Reporter version attributes submitted with the component: purpose, world, outputs[] (name/type/description), requested_limits. Validated by the platform at submission.
attributes.title: Attributes

CoworldReporterWasmReference.title: CoworldReporterWasmReference

cpu.description: Kubernetes CPU limit quantity, e.g. '8'. Caps the container's compute regardless of how many cores the node has, so the runnable behaves the same on every node size. The backend also pins the container's math-library thread pools (OMP/MKL/OpenBLAS/NumExpr) to this many cores. Omit for no limit (the container may burst to the whole node).
cpu.title: Cpu

cpu.description: Kubernetes CPU request quantity, e.g. '2' or '500m'. Omit to use the role default.
cpu.title: Cpu

memory.description: Kubernetes memory request quantity, e.g. '512Mi' or '4Gi'. Omit to use the role default.
memory.title: Memory

ephemeral-storage.description: Kubernetes node-local scratch disk request, e.g. '10Gi'. Covers the unpacked image plus workdir. Omit to use the role default.
ephemeral-storage.title: Ephemeral-Storage

requests.description: Scheduler reservation for this runnable. The backend clamps each field to the role's bounds and rejects an upload whose request exceeds a role maximum. Omit to take the role defaults — e.g. a limits-only profile that just caps CPU.

limits.description: Optional hard compute ceiling. Today only the player role honors a CPU limit; declaring one caps every player pod at that many cores and pins its thread pools to match. Clamped to the role's limit bounds by the backend.

type.description: Role contract implemented by this runnable.
type.title: Type

image.description: Docker image reference to run for this role.
image.title: Image

run.description: Optional process command overriding the image entrypoint or default command.
run.title: Run

env.description: Public environment variables passed to the runnable. Do not put secrets here.
env.title: Env

source_url.description: Optional public source repository, directory, or file URL for this runnable.
source_url.title: Source Url

resources.description: Optional compute request profile for this runnable. Omit to inherit the role's default profile. Declared requests are clamped to per-role bounds by the backend.

type.description: Document reference kind for inline text.
type.title: Type

type.description: Document reference kind for a public HTTP(S) document.
type.title: Type

value.description: Public HTTP(S) URL for the document.
value.title: Value

id.description: Stable variant identifier used by CLIs and league configuration.
id.title: Id

game_config.description: Token-free game config that validates against game.config_schema.
game_config.title: Game Config

description: Schema for a complete game world in the Softmax universe.

$schema.description: Optional JSON Schema URI for IDE tooling.
$schema.title: $Schema

tags.description: Tags describing the Coworld for discovery and classification. When present, at least three are required.
tags.title: Tags

game.description: Game runnable and protocol metadata. Role docs: docs/roles/GAME.md.
game.markdownDescription: See the [Game role](docs/roles/GAME.md) documentation for this role's contract.

player.description: Bundled player runnables. Role docs: docs/roles/PLAYER.md.
player.markdownDescription: See the [Player role](docs/roles/PLAYER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
player.title: Player

reporter.description: Reporter references (spec 0061): platform reporter versions, or wasm components this package builds and submits at upload. Optional; there are no default reporters. Role docs: docs/roles/REPORTER.md.
reporter.markdownDescription: See the [Reporter role](docs/roles/REPORTER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
reporter.title: Reporter

commissioner.description: Commissioner runnables. Optional. Role docs: docs/roles/COMMISSIONER.md.
commissioner.markdownDescription: See the [Commissioner role](docs/roles/COMMISSIONER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
commissioner.title: Commissioner

grader.description: Grader runnables. Optional; include entries when the Coworld ships grader containers. Role docs: docs/roles/GRADER.md.
grader.markdownDescription: See the [Grader role](docs/roles/GRADER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
grader.title: Grader

diagnoser.$comment: Optional in the current schema; intended to become required as this role stabilizes.
diagnoser.description: Diagnoser runnables. Optional today, but expected to become required once the diagnoser contract stabilizes. Role docs: docs/roles/DIAGNOSER.md.
diagnoser.markdownDescription: See the [Diagnoser role](docs/roles/DIAGNOSER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
diagnoser.title: Diagnoser

optimizer.$comment: Optional in the current schema; intended to become required as this role stabilizes.
optimizer.description: Optimizer runnables. Optional today, but expected to become required once the optimizer contract stabilizes. Role docs: docs/roles/OPTIMIZER.md.
optimizer.markdownDescription: See the [Optimizer role](docs/roles/OPTIMIZER.md) documentation for this role's contract.
optimizer.title: Optimizer

certification.description: Smoke-test episode used by coworld certify and default local episode runs.

players_per_user.description: Maximum number of simultaneously active policy memberships a single user may hold in this Coworld's league. Unset means the platform default applies.
players_per_user.title: Players Per User

episode_timeout_minutes.description: Maximum wall-clock minutes a hosted episode of this Coworld may run before the platform kills it. Also bounds hosted certification runs. Must be between 1 and 100. Unset means the platform default (20 minutes).
episode_timeout_minutes.title: Episode Timeout Minutes

title: Coworld Manifest
